NKJV.1: Let a man so consider us, as servants of Christ and stewards of the mysteries of God.
NKJV.2: Moreover it is required in stewards that one be found faithful.
NKJV.3: But with me it is a very small thing that I should be judged by you or by a human court. In fact, I do not even judge myself.
NKJV.4: For I know of nothing against myself, yet I am not justified by this; but He who judges me is the Lord.
NKJV.5: Therefore judge nothing before the time, until the Lord comes, who will both bring to light the hidden things of darkness and reveal the counsels of the hearts. Then each one’s praise will come from God.
NKJV.6: Now these things, brethren, I have figuratively transferred to myself and Apollos for your sakes, that you may learn in us not to think beyond what is written, that none of you may be puffed up on behalf of one against the other.
NKJV.7: For who makes you differ from another? And what do you have that you did not receive? Now if you did indeed receive it, why do you boast as if you had not received it?
NKJV.8: You are already full! You are already rich! You have reigned as kings without us—and indeed I could wish you did reign, that we also might reign with you!
NKJV.9: For I think that God has displayed us, the apostles, last, as men condemned to death; for we have been made a spectacle to the world, both to angels and to men.
NKJV.10: We are fools for Christ’s sake, but you are wise in Christ! We are weak, but you are strong! You are distinguished, but we are dishonored!
NKJV.11: To the present hour we both hunger and thirst, and we are poorly clothed, and beaten, and homeless.
NKJV.12: And we labor, working with our own hands. Being reviled, we bless; being persecuted, we endure;
NKJV.13: being defamed, we entreat. We have been made as the filth of the world, the offscouring of all things until now.
NKJV.14: I do not write these things to shame you, but as my beloved children I warn you.
NKJV.15: For though you might have ten thousand instructors in Christ, yet you do not have many fathers; for in Christ Jesus I have begotten you through the gospel.
NKJV.16: Therefore I urge you, imitate me.
NKJV.17: For this reason I have sent Timothy to you, who is my beloved and faithful son in the Lord, who will remind you of my ways in Christ, as I teach everywhere in every church.
NKJV.18: Now some are puffed up, as though I were not coming to you.
NKJV.19: But I will come to you shortly, if the Lord wills, and I will know, not the word of those who are puffed up, but the power.
NKJV.20: For the kingdom of God is not in word but in power.
NKJV.21: What do you want? Shall I come to you with a rod, or in love and a spirit of gentleness?

ESV.1: This is how one should regard us, as servants of Christ and stewards of the mysteries of God.
ESV.2: Moreover, it is required of stewards that they be found faithful.
ESV.3: But with me it is a very small thing that I should be judged by you or by any human court. In fact, I do not even judge myself.
ESV.4: For I am not aware of anything against myself, but I am not thereby acquitted. It is the Lord who judges me.
ESV.5: Therefore do not pronounce judgment before the time, before the Lord comes, who will bring to light the things now hidden in darkness and will disclose the purposes of the heart. Then each one will receive his commendation from God.
ESV.6: I have applied all these things to myself and Apollos for your benefit, brothers,  that you may learn by us not to go beyond what is written, that none of you may be puffed up in favor of one against another.
ESV.7: For who sees anything different in you? What do you have that you did not receive? If then you received it, why do you boast as if you did not receive it?
ESV.8: Already you have all you want! Already you have become rich! Without us you have become kings! And would that you did reign, so that we might share the rule with you!
ESV.9: For I think that God has exhibited us apostles as last of all, like men sentenced to death, because we have become a spectacle to the world, to angels, and to men.
ESV.10: We are fools for Christ's sake, but you are wise in Christ. We are weak, but you are strong. You are held in honor, but we in disrepute.
ESV.11: To the present hour we hunger and thirst, we are poorly dressed and buffeted and homeless,
ESV.12: and we labor, working with our own hands. When reviled, we bless; when persecuted, we endure;
ESV.13: when slandered, we entreat. We have become, and are still, like the scum of the world, the refuse of all things.
ESV.14: I do not write these things to make you ashamed, but to admonish you as my beloved children.
ESV.15: For though you have countless  guides in Christ, you do not have many fathers. For I became your father in Christ Jesus through the gospel.
ESV.16: I urge you, then, be imitators of me.
ESV.17: That is why I sent  you Timothy, my beloved and faithful child in the Lord, to remind you of my ways in Christ,  as I teach them everywhere in every church.
ESV.18: Some are arrogant, as though I were not coming to you.
ESV.19: But I will come to you soon, if the Lord wills, and I will find out not the talk of these arrogant people but their power.
ESV.20: For the kingdom of God does not consist in talk but in power.
ESV.21: What do you wish? Shall I come to you with a rod, or with love in a spirit of gentleness?

NASB.1: Let a man regard us in this manner, as servants of Christ and stewards of the mysteries of God.
NASB.2: In this case, moreover, it is required of stewards that one be found trustworthy.
NASB.3: But to me it is a very small thing that I may be examined by you, or by [any] human court; in fact, I do not even examine myself.
NASB.4: For I am conscious of nothing against myself, yet I am not by this acquitted; but the one who examines me is the Lord.
NASB.5: Therefore do not go on passing judgment before the time, [but wait] until the Lord comes who will both bring to light the things hidden in the darkness and disclose the motives of [men's] hearts; and then each man's praise will come to him from God.
NASB.6: Now these things, brethren, I have figuratively applied to myself and Apollos for your sakes, so that in us you may learn not to exceed what is written, so that no one of you will become arrogant in behalf of one against the other.
NASB.7: For who regards you as superior? What do you have that you did not receive? And if you did receive it, why do you boast as if you had not received it?
NASB.8: You are already filled, you have already become rich, you have become kings without us; and indeed, [I] wish that you had become kings so that we also might reign with you.
NASB.9: For, I think, God has exhibited us apostles last of all, as men condemned to death; because we have become a spectacle to the world, both to angels and to men.
NASB.10: We are fools for Christ's sake, but you are prudent in Christ; we are weak, but you are strong; you are distinguished, but we are without honor.
NASB.11: To this present hour we are both hungry and thirsty, and are poorly clothed, and are roughly treated, and are homeless;
NASB.12: and we toil, working with our own hands; when we are reviled, we bless; when we are persecuted, we endure;
NASB.13: when we are slandered, we try to conciliate; we have become as the scum of the world, the dregs of all things, [even] until now.
NASB.14: I do not write these things to shame you, but to admonish you as my beloved children.
NASB.15: For if you were to have countless tutors in Christ, yet [you would] not [have] many fathers, for in Christ Jesus I became your father through the gospel.
NASB.16: Therefore I exhort you, be imitators of me.
NASB.17: For this reason I have sent to you Timothy, who is my beloved and faithful child in the Lord, and he will remind you of my ways which are in Christ, just as I teach everywhere in every church.
NASB.18: Now some have become arrogant, as though I were not coming to you.
NASB.19: But I will come to you soon, if the Lord wills, and I shall find out, not the words of those who are arrogant but their power.
NASB.20: For the kingdom of God does not consist in words but in power.
NASB.21: What do you desire? Shall I come to you with a rod, or with love and a spirit of gentleness?

NLT.1: So look at Apollos and me as mere servants of Christ who have been put in charge of explaining God’s mysteries.
NLT.2: Now, a person who is put in charge as a manager must be faithful.
NLT.3: As for me, it matters very little how I might be evaluated by you or by any human authority. I don’t even trust my own judgment on this point.
NLT.4: My conscience is clear, but that doesn’t prove I’m right. It is the Lord himself who will examine me and decide.
NLT.5: So don’t make judgments about anyone ahead of time — before the Lord returns. For he will bring our darkest secrets to light and will reveal our private motives. Then God will give to each one whatever praise is due.
NLT.6: Dear brothers and sisters, I have used Apollos and myself to illustrate what I’ve been saying. If you pay attention to what I have quoted from the Scriptures, you won’t be proud of one of your leaders at the expense of another.
NLT.7: For what gives you the right to make such a judgment? What do you have that God hasn’t given you? And if everything you have is from God, why boast as though it were not a gift?
NLT.8: You think you already have everything you need. You think you are already rich. You have begun to reign in God’s kingdom without us! I wish you really were reigning already, for then we would be reigning with you.
NLT.9: Instead, I sometimes think God has put us apostles on display, like prisoners of war at the end of a victor’s parade, condemned to die. We have become a spectacle to the entire world — to people and angels alike.
NLT.10: Our dedication to Christ makes us look like fools, but you claim to be so wise in Christ! We are weak, but you are so powerful! You are honored, but we are ridiculed.
NLT.11: Even now we go hungry and thirsty, and we don’t have enough clothes to keep warm. We are often beaten and have no home.
NLT.12: We work wearily with our own hands to earn our living. We bless those who curse us. We are patient with those who abuse us.
NLT.13: We appeal gently when evil things are said about us. Yet we are treated like the world’s garbage, like everybody’s trash — right up to the present moment.
NLT.14: I am not writing these things to shame you, but to warn you as my beloved children.
NLT.15: For even if you had ten thousand others to teach you about Christ, you have only one spiritual father. For I became your father in Christ Jesus when I preached the Good News to you.
NLT.16: So I urge you to imitate me.
NLT.17: That’s why I have sent Timothy, my beloved and faithful child in the Lord. He will remind you of how I follow Christ Jesus, just as I teach in all the churches wherever I go.
NLT.18: Some of you have become arrogant, thinking I will not visit you again.
NLT.19: But I will come — and soon — if the Lord lets me, and then I’ll find out whether these arrogant people just give pretentious speeches or whether they really have God’s power.
NLT.20: For the Kingdom of God is not just a lot of talk; it is living by God’s power.
NLT.21: Which do you choose? Should I come with a rod to punish you, or should I come with love and a gentle spirit?

WEB.1: So let a man think of us as Christ’s servants, and stewards of God’s mysteries.
WEB.2: Here, moreover, it is required of stewards, that they be found faithful.
WEB.3: But with me it is a very small thing that I should be judged by you, or by man’s judgment. Yes, I don’t judge my own self.
WEB.4: For I know nothing against myself. Yet I am not justified by this, but he who judges me is the Lord.
WEB.5: Therefore judge nothing before the time, until the Lord comes, who will both bring to light the hidden things of darkness, and reveal the counsels of the hearts. Then each man will get his praise from God.
WEB.6: Now these things, brothers, I have in a figure transferred to myself and Apollos for your sakes, that in us you might learn not to think beyond the things which are written, that none of you be puffed up against one another.
WEB.7: For who makes you different? And what do you have that you didn’t receive? But if you did receive it, why do you boast as if you had not received it?
WEB.8: You are already filled. You have already become rich. You have come to reign without us. Yes, and I wish that you did reign, that we also might reign with you.
WEB.9: For, I think that God has displayed us, the apostles, last of all, like men sentenced to death. For we are made a spectacle to the world, both to angels and men.
WEB.10: We are fools for Christ’s sake, but you are wise in Christ. We are weak, but you are strong. You have honor, but we have dishonor.
WEB.11: Even to this present hour we hunger, thirst, are naked, are beaten, and have no certain dwelling place.
WEB.12: We toil, working with our own hands. When people curse us, we bless. Being persecuted, we endure.
WEB.13: Being defamed, we entreat. We are made as the filth of the world, the dirt wiped off by all, even until now.
WEB.14: I don’t write these things to shame you, but to admonish you as my beloved children.
WEB.15: For though you have ten thousand tutors in Christ, yet not many fathers. For in Christ Jesus, I became your father through the Good News.
WEB.16: I beg you therefore, be imitators of me.
WEB.17: Because of this I have sent Timothy to you, who is my beloved and faithful child in the Lord, who will remind you of my ways which are in Christ, even as I teach everywhere in every assembly.
WEB.18: Now some are puffed up, as though I were not coming to you.
WEB.19: But I will come to you shortly, if the Lord is willing. And I will know, not the word of those who are puffed up, but the power.
WEB.20: For God’s Kingdom is not in word, but in power.
WEB.21: What do you want? Shall I come to you with a rod, or in love and a spirit of gentleness?

CSB17.1: A person should think of us in this way: as servants <sup>ⓛ</sup> of Christ and managers <sup>ⓜ</sup> of the mysteries of God. <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.2: In this regard, it is required that managers be found faithful. <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.3: It is of little <sup>ⓑ</sup> importance to me that I should be judged <sup>ⓒ</sup> by you or by any human court. <sup>[1]</sup> In fact, I don’t even judge myself.
CSB17.4: For I am not conscious of anything against myself, but I am not justified <sup>ⓓ</sup> by this. It is the Lord who judges me.
CSB17.5: So don’t judge <sup>ⓔ</sup> anything prematurely, before the Lord comes, <sup>ⓕ</sup> who will both bring to light what is hidden in darkness and reveal the intentions of the hearts. And then praise will come to each one from God. <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.6: Now, brothers and sisters, <sup>ⓗ</sup> I have applied these things to myself and Apollos <sup>ⓘ</sup> for your benefit, so that you may learn from us the meaning of the saying: “Nothing beyond what is written.” The purpose is that none of you will be arrogant, favoring one person over another.
CSB17.7: For who makes you so superior? What do you have that you didn’t receive? If, in fact, you did receive it, why do you boast as if you hadn’t received it?
CSB17.8: You are already full! You are already rich! You have begun to reign as kings without us — and I wish you did reign, so that we could also reign with you!
CSB17.9: For I think God has displayed us, the apostles, in last place, like men condemned to die: We have become a spectacle to the world, both to angels and to people.
CSB17.10: We are fools for Christ, but you are wise in Christ! <sup>ⓙ</sup> We are weak, but you are strong! You are distinguished, but we are dishonored!
CSB17.11: Up to the present hour we are both hungry and thirsty; we are poorly clothed, roughly treated, homeless;
CSB17.12: we labor, working <sup>ⓚ</sup> with our own hands. <sup>ⓛ</sup> When we are reviled, we bless; when we are persecuted, we endure it;
CSB17.13: when we are slandered, we respond graciously. Even now, we are like the scum of the earth, like everyone’s garbage. <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.14: I’m not writing this to shame you, but to warn you as my dear children. <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.15: For you may have countless instructors in Christ, but you don’t have many fathers. For I became your father <sup>ⓐ</sup> in Christ Jesus through the gospel.
CSB17.16: Therefore I urge you to imitate me.
CSB17.17: This is why I have sent <sup>ⓑ</sup> Timothy to you. He is my dearly loved and faithful <sup>ⓒ</sup> child in the Lord. He will remind you about my ways in Christ Jesus, just as I teach everywhere in every church.
CSB17.18: Now some are arrogant, as though I were not coming to you.
CSB17.19: But I will come to you soon, <sup>ⓓ</sup> if the Lord wills, and I will find out not the talk, but the power of those who are arrogant.
CSB17.20: For the kingdom of God is not a matter of talk but of power.
CSB17.21: What do you want? Should I come to you with a rod, <sup>ⓔ</sup> or in love and a spirit of gentleness? <sup>ⓕ</sup>

AMP.1: So then, let us [who minister] be regarded as servants of Christ and stewards (trustees, administrators) of the mysteries of God [that He chooses to reveal].
AMP.2: In this case, moreover, it is required [as essential and demanded] of stewards that one be found faithful and trustworthy.
AMP.3: But [as for me personally] it matters very little to me that I may be judged by you or any human court [on this point]; in fact, I do not even judge myself.
AMP.4: I am aware of nothing against myself and I feel blameless, but I am not by this acquitted [before God]. It is the Lord who judges me.
AMP.5: So do not go on passing judgment before the appointed time, but wait until the Lord comes, for He will both bring to light the [secret] things that are hidden in darkness and disclose the motives of the hearts. Then each one's praise will come from God.
AMP.6: Now I have applied these things [that is, the analogies about factions] to myself and Apollos for your benefit, believers, so that you may learn from us not to go beyond what is written [in Scripture], so that none of you will become arrogant and boast in favor of one [minister or teacher] against the other.
AMP.7: For who regards you as superior or what sets you apart as special? What do you have that you did not receive [from another]? And if in fact you received it [from God or someone else], why do you boast as if you had not received it [but had gained it by yourself]?
AMP.8: [You behave as if] you are already filled [with spiritual wisdom and in need of nothing more]. Already you have become rich [in spiritual gifts]! You [in your conceit] have ascended your thrones and become kings without us; and how I wish [that it were true and] that you did reign as kings, so that we might reign with you.
AMP.9: For, I think, God has exhibited us apostles at the end of the line, like men sentenced to death [and paraded as prisoners in a procession], because we have become a spectacle to the world [a show in the world's amphitheater], both to angels and to men.
AMP.10: We are [regarded as] fools for Christ, but you are so wise in Christ; we are weak, but you are strong; you are highly esteemed, but we are dishonored.
AMP.11: To this present hour we are both hungry and thirsty; we are continually poorly dressed, and we are roughly treated, and wander homeless.
AMP.12: We work [for our living], working hard with our own hands. When we are reviled and verbally abused, we bless. When we are persecuted, we take it patiently and endure.
AMP.13: When we are slandered, we try to be conciliatory and answer softly. We have become like the scum of the world, the dregs of all things, even until now.
AMP.14: I do not write these things to shame you, but to warn and advise you as my beloved children.
AMP.15: For even if you were to have ten thousand teachers [to guide you] in Christ, yet you would not have many fathers [who led you to Christ and assumed responsibility for you], for I became your father in Christ Jesus through the good news [of salvation].
AMP.16: So I urge you, be imitators of me [just as a child imitates his father].
AMP.17: For this reason I have sent Timothy to you, who is my beloved and faithful child in the Lord, and he will remind you of my way of life in Christ [my conduct and my precepts for godly living], just as I teach everywhere in every church.
AMP.18: Now some of you have become arrogant and pretentious, as though I were not coming to see you.
AMP.19: But I will come to you soon, if the Lord is willing, and I will find out not just the talk of these arrogant people, but [evaluate] their [spiritual] power [whether they live up to their own claims].
AMP.20: For the kingdom of God is not based on talk but on power.
AMP.21: Which do you prefer? Shall I come to you with a rod [of discipline and correction], or with love and a gentle spirit?

MSG.1: Don't imagine us leaders to be something we aren't. We are servants of Christ, not his masters. We are guides into God's most sublime secrets, not security guards posted to protect them.
MSG.2: The requirements for a good guide are reliability and accurate knowledge.
MSG.3: It matters very little to me what you think of me, even less where I rank in popular opinion. I don't even rank myself. Comparisons in these matters are pointless.
MSG.4: I'm not aware of anything that would disqualify me from being a good guide for you, but that doesn't mean much. The Master makes that judgment.
MSG.5: So don't get ahead of the Master and jump to conclusions with your judgments before all the evidence is in. When he comes, he will bring out in the open and place in evidence all kinds of things we never even dreamed of—inner motives and purposes and prayers. Only then will any one of us get to hear the "Well done!" of God.
MSG.6: All I'm doing right now, friends, is showing how these things pertain to Apollos and me so that you will learn restraint and not rush into making judgments without knowing all the facts. It's important to look at things from God's point of view. I would rather not see you inflating or deflating reputations based on mere hearsay.
MSG.7: For who do you know that really knows you, knows your heart? And even if they did, is there anything they would discover in you that you could take credit for? Isn't everything you have and everything you are sheer gifts from God? So what's the point of all this comparing and competing?
MSG.8: You already have all you need. You already have more access to God than you can handle. Without bringing either Apollos or me into it, you're sitting on top of the world—at least God's world—and we're right there, sitting alongside you!
MSG.9: It seems to me that God has put us who bear his Message on stage in a theater in which no one wants to buy a ticket. We're something everyone stands around and stares at, like an accident in the street.
MSG.10: We're the Messiah's misfits. You might be sure of yourselves, but we live in the midst of frailties and uncertainties. You might be well-thought-of by others, but we're mostly kicked around.
MSG.11: Much of the time we don't have enough to eat, we wear patched and threadbare clothes, we get doors slammed in our faces,
MSG.12: and we pick up odd jobs anywhere we can to eke out a living. When they call us names, we say, "God bless you."
MSG.13: When they spread rumors about us, we put in a good word for them. We're treated like garbage, potato peelings from the culture's kitchen. And it's not getting any better.
MSG.14: I'm not writing all this as a neighborhood scold just to make you feel rotten. I'm writing as a father to you, my children. I love you and want you to grow up well, not spoiled.
MSG.15: There are a lot of people around who can't wait to tell you what you've done wrong, but there aren't many fathers willing to take the time and effort to help you grow up. It was as Jesus helped me proclaim God's Message to you that I became your father.
MSG.16: I'm not, you know, asking you to do anything I'm not already doing myself.
MSG.17: This is why I sent Timothy to you earlier. He is also my dear son, and true to the Master. He will refresh your memory on the instructions I regularly give all the churches on the way of Christ.
MSG.18: I know there are some among you who are so full of themselves they never listen to anyone, let alone me. They don't think I'll ever show up in person.
MSG.19: But I'll be there sooner than you think, God willing, and then we'll see if they're full of anything but hot air.
MSG.20: God's Way is not a matter of mere talk; it's an empowered life.
MSG.21: So how should I prepare to come to you? As a severe disciplinarian who makes you toe the mark? Or as a good friend and counselor who wants to share heart-to-heart with you? You decide.

NIV.1:
Apostles of Christ
So then, men ought to regard us as servants of Christ and as those entrusted with the secret things of God.
NIV.2: Now it is required that those who have been given a trust must prove faithful.
NIV.3: I care very little if I am judged by you or by any human court; indeed, I do not even judge myself.
NIV.4: My conscience is clear, but that does not make me innocent. It is the Lord who judges me.
NIV.5: Therefore judge nothing before the appointed time; wait till the Lord comes. He will bring to light what is hidden in darkness and will expose the motives of men’s hearts. At that time each will receive his praise from God.
NIV.6: Now, brothers, I have applied these things to myself and Apollos for your benefit, so that you may learn from us the meaning of the saying, “Do not go beyond what is written.” Then you will not take pride in one man over against another.
NIV.7: For who makes you different from anyone else? What do you have that you did not receive? And if you did receive it, why do you boast as though you did not?
NIV.8: Already you have all you want! Already you have become rich! You have become kings—and that without us! How I wish that you really had become kings so that we might be kings with you!
NIV.9: For it seems to me that God has put us apostles on display at the end of the procession, like men condemned to die in the arena. We have been made a spectacle to the whole universe, to angels as well as to men.
NIV.10: We are fools for Christ, but you are so wise in Christ! We are weak, but you are strong! You are honored, we are dishonored!
NIV.11: To this very hour we go hungry and thirsty, we are in rags, we are brutally treated, we are homeless.
NIV.12: We work hard with our own hands. When we are cursed, we bless; when we are persecuted, we endure it;
NIV.13: when we are slandered, we answer kindly. Up to this moment we have become the scum of the earth, the refuse of the world.
NIV.14: I am not writing this to shame you, but to warn you, as my dear children.
NIV.15: Even though you have ten thousand guardians in Christ, you do not have many fathers, for in Christ Jesus I became your father through the gospel.
NIV.16: Therefore I urge you to imitate me.
NIV.17: For this reason I am sending to you Timothy, my son whom I love, who is faithful in the Lord. He will remind you of my way of life in Christ Jesus, which agrees with what I teach everywhere in every church.
NIV.18: Some of you have become arrogant, as if I were not coming to you.
NIV.19: But I will come to you very soon, if the Lord is willing, and then I will find out not only how these arrogant people are talking, but what power they have.
NIV.20: For the kingdom of God is not a matter of talk but of power.
NIV.21: What do you prefer? Shall I come to you with a whip, or in love and with a gentle spirit?

NRSVCE.1: Think of us in this way, as servants of Christ and stewards of God’s mysteries.
NRSVCE.2: Moreover, it is required of stewards that they be found trustworthy.
NRSVCE.3: But with me it is a very small thing that I should be judged by you or by any human court. I do not even judge myself.
NRSVCE.4: I am not aware of anything against myself, but I am not thereby acquitted. It is the Lord who judges me.
NRSVCE.5: Therefore do not pronounce judgment before the time, before the Lord comes, who will bring to light the things now hidden in darkness and will disclose the purposes of the heart. Then each one will receive commendation from God.
NRSVCE.6: I have applied all this to Apollos and myself for your benefit, brothers and sisters, so that you may learn through us the meaning of the saying, “Nothing beyond what is written,” so that none of you will be puffed up in favor of one against another.
NRSVCE.7: For who sees anything different in you? What do you have that you did not receive? And if you received it, why do you boast as if it were not a gift?
NRSVCE.8: Already you have all you want! Already you have become rich! Quite apart from us you have become kings! Indeed, I wish that you had become kings, so that we might be kings with you!
NRSVCE.9: For I think that God has exhibited us apostles as last of all, as though sentenced to death, because we have become a spectacle to the world, to angels and to mortals.
NRSVCE.10: We are fools for the sake of Christ, but you are wise in Christ. We are weak, but you are strong. You are held in honor, but we in disrepute.
NRSVCE.11: To the present hour we are hungry and thirsty, we are poorly clothed and beaten and homeless,
NRSVCE.12: and we grow weary from the work of our own hands. When reviled, we bless; when persecuted, we endure;
NRSVCE.13: when slandered, we speak kindly. We have become like the rubbish of the world, the dregs of all things, to this very day.
NRSVCE.14: I am not writing this to make you ashamed, but to admonish you as my beloved children.
NRSVCE.15: For though you might have ten thousand guardians in Christ, you do not have many fathers. Indeed, in Christ Jesus I became your father through the gospel.
NRSVCE.16: I appeal to you, then, be imitators of me.
NRSVCE.17: For this reason I sent you Timothy, who is my beloved and faithful child in the Lord, to remind you of my ways in Christ Jesus, as I teach them everywhere in every church.
NRSVCE.18: But some of you, thinking that I am not coming to you, have become arrogant.
NRSVCE.19: But I will come to you soon, if the Lord wills, and I will find out not the talk of these arrogant people but their power.
NRSVCE.20: For the kingdom of God depends not on talk but on power.
NRSVCE.21: What would you prefer? Am I to come to you with a stick, or with love in a spirit of gentleness?

NET.1: One should think about us this way — as servants of Christ and stewards of the mysteries of God.
NET.2: Now what is sought in stewards is that one be found faithful.
NET.3: So for me, it is a minor matter that I am judged by you or by any human court. In fact, I do not even judge myself.
NET.4: For I am not aware of anything against myself, but I am not acquitted because of this. The one who judges me is the Lord.
NET.5: So then, do not judge anything before the time. Wait until the Lord comes. He will bring to light the hidden things of darkness and reveal the motives of hearts. Then each will receive recognition from God.
NET.6: I have applied these things to myself and Apollos because of you, brothers and sisters, so that through us you may learn “not to go beyond what is written,” so that none of you will be puffed up in favor of the one against the other.
NET.7: For who concedes you any superiority? What do you have that you did not receive? And if you received it, why do you boast as though you did not?
NET.8: Already you are satisfied! Already you are rich! You have become kings without us! I wish you had become kings so that we could reign with you!
NET.9: For, I think, God has exhibited us apostles last of all, as men condemned to die, because we have become a spectacle to the world, both to angels and to people.
NET.10: We are fools for Christ, but you are wise in Christ! We are weak, but you are strong! You are distinguished, we are dishonored!
NET.11: To the present hour we are hungry and thirsty, poorly clothed, brutally treated, and without a roof over our heads.
NET.12: We do hard work, toiling with our own hands. When we are verbally abused, we respond with a blessing, when persecuted, we endure,
NET.13: when people lie about us, we answer in a friendly manner. We are the world’s dirt and scum, even now.
NET.14: I am not writing these things to shame you, but to correct you as my dear children.
NET.15: For though you may have ten thousand guardians in Christ, you do not have many fathers, because I became your father in Christ Jesus through the gospel.
NET.16: I encourage you, then, be imitators of me.
NET.17: For this reason, I have sent Timothy to you, who is my dear and faithful son in the Lord. He will remind you of my ways in Christ, as I teach them everywhere in every church.
NET.18: Some have become arrogant, as if I were not coming to you.
NET.19: But I will come to you soon, if the Lord is willing, and I will find out not only the talk of these arrogant people, but also their power.
NET.20: For the kingdom of God is demonstrated not in idle talk but with power.
NET.21: What do you want? Shall I come to you with a rod of discipline or with love and a spirit of gentleness?
